provenance.review_method: Model-assisted structural review of every supplied evidence note; no systematic full-text verification of the cited corpus

evidence.title: Pandemic emerged in Wuhan, home to the world's leading bat-SARS-coronavirus lab, ~1,500-1,800 km from the nearest known viral reservoir
evidence.claim: A novel SARS-related coronavirus first erupted in Wuhan, the city hosting the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV) — the world's leading collector and laboratory manipulator of SARS-like bat coronaviruses — even though the closest known wild relatives circulate in horseshoe bats in Yunnan (China) and Laos, roughly 1,500-1,800 km away from Wuhan. Lab-leak proponents argue this geographic juxtaposition is an extraordinary coincidence.
evidence.evidence_quality:
The component facts are strong and primary-sourced: WIV's collection (peer-reviewed virology literature, institutional records), RaTG13's Mojiang provenance and distance (Zhou et al. 2020 Nature; reported distances), and BANAL-52's Laos provenance (Temmam et al. 2022, Nature, peer-reviewed). So the *factual substrate* — virus in Wuhan, top lab in Wuhan, reservoir ~1,500-1,800 km away — is robust (confidence high).

However, the *inferential weight* is circumstantial. The claim describes a spatial coincidence; it contains no genomic, documentary, or epidemiological evidence that the lab was the actual source. It is the kind of evidence that constrains priors rather than identifies a mechanism.
evidence.strongest_counterargument:
The proximity is real but weakly diagnostic, for several reasons (Wikipedia "COVID-19 lab leak theory"; Lewandowsky et al.):
- **Labs cluster where people cluster.** Most large Chinese cities host coronavirus research laboratories; Wuhan (~11 million people) is a major transport, trade, and wildlife-trade hub. Outbreaks of bat-origin viruses "typically begin in rural areas but are first noticed in large cities," so any spillover plausibly surfaces in a big surveilled city regardless of labs.
- **Conjunction fallacy.** Lewandowsky and colleagues argue treating the lab's location as *a priori* evidence is "literally a coincidence" reasoning — lab-leak proponents foreground the WIV while ignoring the equally-present wet markets known to be spillover venues.
- **Positive natural-origin evidence at the same site.** Spatial-epidemiology analyses (Worobey et al. 2022, Science; Pekar et al. 2022, Science) place the earliest cases and environmental positives at the Huanan Seafood Market, where live wild mammals susceptible to SARS-CoV-2 were sold — a competing, mechanism-bearing explanation for *why Wuhan*. If the market is the epicenter, the WIV's presence becomes incidental.
- WIV's main campus is ~12 km from the Huanan market; the within-city co-location is not itself unusual for a metropolis.

In short: proximity establishes that a leak was *possible*, but a wildlife-trade spillover in a major hub city is at least as consistent with the same geography.
evidence.confidence_assessment:
- **Claim confidence:** 0.9 — The underlying facts (novel SARS-CoV in Wuhan; WIV as the top bat-CoV lab there; nearest known relatives ~1,500-1,800 km away in Yunnan/Laos) are well-sourced and largely uncontested. The minor uncertainty is wording: "1,500-1,900 km" is approximate, the true ancestor/reservoir location is unknown, and "closest relatives" is a moving target (RaTG13 → BANAL-52).
- **Support strength:** weak — for the lab-leak hypothesis. As pure circumstance it raises the lab-leak prior modestly but is symmetrically explainable by natural spillover in a dense trade/travel hub, and is undercut by positive market-centered epidemiological evidence. It is suggestive, not dispositive.
- **Net:** A calibrated reasoner should accept the geographic coincidence as true and genuinely notable, while recognizing it is opportunity-only evidence that moves the needle only slightly absent a mechanism — and that the same city-level geography is independently predicted by the wildlife-trade hypothesis.

citations.citation: Antonio Regalado (2022). "Meet the scientist at the center of the covid lab leak controversy" (Shi Zhengli profile). MIT Technology Review. https://www.technologyreview.com/2022/02/09/1044985/shi-zhengli-covid-lab-leak-wuhan/
Wikipedia contributors. "COVID-19 lab leak theory" (proximity argument and standard rebuttal; Lewandowsky conjunction-fallacy framing). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_lab_leak_theory
Wikipedia contributors. "Wuhan Institute of Virology" (300+ bat CoV sequences; BSL-4; Yunnan sampling). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wuhan_Institute_of_Virology
Wikipedia contributors. "RaTG13" (96.1% identity; Mojiang/Yunnan provenance; ~1,600 km from Wuhan; later BANAL matches). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RaTG13

Temmam, S., Vongphayloth, K., Baquero, E., et al. (2022). "Bat coronaviruses related to SARS-CoV-2 and infectious for human cells." Nature 604, 330-336 (BANAL-52, Feuang, Laos; 96.8% identity; ACE2 binding). https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-04532-4

Worobey, M., et al. (2022). "The Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market in Wuhan was the early epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic." Science 377, 951-959 (competing market-centered geography). https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.abp8715

framing_assumptions: Wuhan is selected after the event as a city unusually associated with bat-coronavirus research, making the co-location appear diagnostically rare.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on the reference class of cities, labs, outbreak-detection hubs, wildlife trade routes, and whether location hypotheses were specified in advance.
coverage_gaps: No quantified comparison estimates the probability of Wuhan under laboratory and natural-trade pathways using symmetric geographic models.
correlation_risks: Correlated with WIV capability and biosafety evidence, while competing market geography is represented by several overlapping items.
capability_limits: Circumstantial patterns are especially sensitive to base rates, alternative explanations, and selection of which coincidences are noticed.

evidential: The co-location is real; its likelihood ratio is not established.
framing_conditional: Choosing the lab, market, city, or regional trade network as the salient geographic unit changes the apparent coincidence.

notes: A clear example of reference-class and salience choices entering before assessment.
This is a substantive structural annotation of the supplied evidence note, not an independent finding that its factual claim is true.
